title: oxalis pulchella var. pulchella
intro: Oxalis pulchella var. pulchella, commonly known as the Violet Wood Sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a natural diuretic and to help lower blood pressure due to its high levels of oxalic acid. The health benefits of Oxalis pulchella var. pulchella include its potential to help treat conditions such as arthritis and gout, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity.
